text_instruction: Horizontally flip the structure of the binary tree, swapping the positions of all left and right sibling nodes.
Fill the rectangular leaf nodes with a light gray color to distinguish them from the circular internal nodes.
Swap the positions of the 'I:18' leaf node and the '19' subtree under the parent node '37'.
Increase the horizontal distance separating the '(a) Before' diagram from the '(b) After' diagram.
Remove the text label 'Sink' displayed next to the top node.
Apply a light gray fill to the accepting state node q2 in every step of the diagram.
Invert the entire flow of the diagram by reversing the direction of all arrowheads so they point from Box 2 to Box 1.
Reposition the circle labeled '1' downwards to reduce the vertical whitespace between it and the node labeled '3'.
Change the solid connecting lines in the trees to be dashed lines.
Convert all the dashed blue connector lines to solid lines.
Modify the geometry of the central 'Computer Program' box by changing it from a standard rectangle to a rounded rectangle.
Delete the gray background shading behind the 'Mapper' column on the left side.
Swap the horizontal positions of the "Reporting" and "Archive" blocks within the HIS dashed container.
Connect the bottom-left and bottom-right nodes with a new edge.
Refine the partition structure by doubling the number of rectangular steps from five to ten.
Extend the right-hand branch by adding a new child node labeled 'd' connected to node 'c'.
Shift the position of the second syntax tree structure further to the right to increase the horizontal spacing between the two diagrams.
Reflect the entire binary tree horizontally to create its mirror image, effectively swapping the left and right subtrees for every node.
Move the label 'F' to the left side of the point, placing it inside the square.
Swap the entire left branch rooted at node 30 with the right branch rooted at node 20.
Shift the blue grouping box on the far right, along with its contained nodes, slightly to the right to create more separation from the preceding column.
Rename the input label 'x' on the far left to 'u'.
Insert a new circular node containing the value '2' as the left child of the node labeled '18' in the upper tree diagram.
Delete the circular node labeled 'six' located at the top right of the graph.
Remove the extraneous text label 'help!' located to the left of the branch connecting node 1 and node 4.
Reconfigure the diagram structure to define the highlighted 'tile' as the first 5 vertical columns, rather than the top 4 horizontal rows.
Delete the small circular node labeled 'Child 2-1' found on the left branch.
Annotate the diamond shape in the right-hand diagram by adding the label 'Check' directly below it.
Delete the curved arrow labeled '0' that transitions from state B back to state A.
Append a new rectangular node labeled 'decision 4' connected to the 'Check D' diamond to handle the 'accept D' path.
Invert the entire logical flow of the diagram by reversing the direction of every arrow, making the process flow backwards from 'e' to 'a' and from 'd' to '2'.
Append a new blue box labeled 'TSemiColon' to the right of the final TId block.
Remove the caption 'Figura 1: A figure with multiple subfigures' located at the bottom of the page.
Change the shape of the yellow 'End' node to a rounded square.
Interchange the positions of the entire 'Reduced Model' workflow on the left and the 'Full Model' workflow on the right.
Delete the 'expert' oval located at the top left of the chart.
Shift the position of the top node '0000' upwards to increase the vertical gap between it and the next row of nodes.
Delete the isolated box labeled 'A7. n is prime' located at the bottom right of the flowchart.
Delete the final step 'E. Some text' from the diagram.
Update the label inside the second filter box from 'Filter process 2' to 'Artifact Removal'.
Replace the text label "Longer Text D" in the central black block with "Primary Node".
Shift the label 'b' on the vertical transition arrow from state q_0/epsilon to q_3/0 to the left side of the line.
Change the line style of the transition arrow going from state q2/1 to q'0/1 to be dashed.
Shift the 'Monomorphism' node further to the left to create more horizontal separation from the 'Polymorphism' structure.
Relocate the main caption 'Figure 1' from the bottom to the top center of the diagram.
Shift the entire second row of process blocks downwards to increase the vertical gap between the top and bottom sections of the flow.
Truncate the diagram's hierarchy by deleting the entire bottom row of outcomes and their connecting lines, preserving only the root 'ABC' and the second level of nodes.
Reverse the direction of the vertical arrows so that they point from the observed variables x to the latent variables z.
Delete the text label 'M' found above the rightmost rectangle.
Swap the positions of the entire Teammate1 branch with the Teammate5 branch.
Remove the elliptical node labeled 'y' found at the far right of the diagram.
Increase the horizontal separation between the nodes Q, R, and S.
Change the line style of the arrow leading from the 'Pipeline' box to the 'PDN' node to be dashed.
Change the line type of the curved arrow labeled '$S_1 F_2$' to dashed.
Reposition the 'Input process' text label so that it sits directly above the square box labeled 'I' instead of below it.
Add a new item reading '- Information I' to the list of variables situated directly above the 'Inputs' box.
Reorganize the leftmost K-map by transposing the variables, placing 'cd' on the vertical axis and 'ab' on the horizontal axis.
Shift the rightmost triangular node 'Z' closer to the root node 'X' to reduce the horizontal spacing between them.
Place a red dot marker at the coordinate (0, -1) on the first circle graph to explicitly indicate its center.
Add a curved arrow connecting the top of the yellow circle at index 1 to the top of the yellow circle at index 3.
Invert the control flow logic at the 'Test' decision node by swapping the 'Vrai' and 'Faux' branches so the loop continues on False and breaks on True.
Increase the horizontal gap between the middle subgraph and the rightmost subgraph to better distinguish the separate components.
Remove the long arrow going from the top of the S box to the V box.
Swap the positions of the 'Module 1' and 'Module 2' blocks so that 'Module 2' sits on top of 'Module 1'.
Increase the thickness of all the lines connecting the upper and lower nodes.
Change the solid arrow connecting node C5 to node C3 into a dashed line.
Add a directed arrow pointing from node $C_2$ to node $C_5$.
Convert the shape of the 'Reset' node from a circle to a square.
Shift the large yellow container on the right further to the right to create more separation from the central reaction node.
Change the fill color of the nodes currently highlighted in green to a soft orange.
Draw a straight edge connecting node 1 and node 3.
Modify the red nodes (1, 3, 6, and 8) by changing their shape from circles to squares.
Add a column header labeled "Stage 5" above the rightmost vertical group of nodes.
Flip the direction of all the arrows so the data flows from the bottom Sink up to the Source.
Append a new oval node labeled 'Loyalty' to the right of the 'Satisfaction' node.
Simplify the diagram's topology by removing all self-transition loops from the nodes.
Delete the rectangle labeled 'Document_d' located at the bottom of the right-hand column.
Add a red arrow pointing from the 'Document A' box on the left to the 'Document a' box in the middle column.
Insert a new yellow rectangle labeled 'Task 5' directly below 'Task 4' in the column for Goal 2, Activity 2.
Move the 'foobar' node to the left so that it is positioned directly below the 'Hello World' node.
Draw a new undirected edge connecting node B and node E.
Delete the node labeled 'one13' from the Level 3 row.
Delete the red curved arrow representing the transition from state 'n+1' to state 'n'.
Change the style of all the connecting arrows from solid lines to dashed lines.
Restructure the hierarchy so that 'test3' and 'test4' become child nodes of 'test1' instead of branching directly from 'test0'.

Convert the diagram into a simpler 2x2 square matrix multiplication example by removing the bottom two rows of Matrix A, the third column of Matrix B, and the corresponding sections of the result matrix and legend.
Delete the leaf node containing 'Test text e' under the 'Central' category.
Insert a centered title at the top of the diagram that reads 'Signal Processing Inputs'.
Move the state labeled '4' and its adjacent text label further down to increase the vertical gap between it and state '3'.
Invert the logical flow of the entire network by reversing the direction of every black arrow.
Swap the positions of the 'Structure 1' container and the 'Structure 2' container, placing the green panel on the left and the blue panel on the right.
Delete the green node containing the ellipsis at the bottom of the right-hand column.
Change the border color of all the inner boxes labeled 'Description 1' through 'Description 6' from orange to black.
Add a new circular node labeled 'J' to the right of node 'c'.
Swap the horizontal positions of the 'NFC' and 'Camera' nodes.
Convert the shape of the top node labeled 'a' into a square.
Delete the self-loop arrow labeled p22 found on state 2.
Remove the dashed horizontal line that separates the Latent and Observed rows.
Change the border of the top 'Requested size' rectangle to be a dashed line.
Change the shape of the central node in Figure 2 to a square.
Add a new grey circular node connected to the bottom of the central vertical chain.
Restructure the probability tree to model a 4-sided die instead of a 6-sided one by removing the branches and outcomes for 5 and 6.
Move the 'single instance' component to the far right of the diagram, positioning it after the large 'multi instance' stack.
Transform the logic of the finite automaton to its complement by changing the accepting state S4 to a regular state, and promoting the regular states S1, S2, and S3 to accepting states.
Remove the descriptive text paragraph situated beneath the leftmost node labeled 'Text Text'.
Invert the logical flow of the classification process by swapping the 'yes' and 'no' branches for every decision diamond in the diagram.
Add a bold title 'Multi-Layer Perceptron Architecture' centered at the very top of the diagram.
Swap the positions of node A and node B.
Move the top row of circular nodes further up to increase the vertical spacing between them and the bottom rectangular nodes.
Eliminate the text 'white' from the central intersection area.
Move the vertical vector containing the y terms to the right side of the matrix labeled A.
Invert the flow of the hierarchical tree on the right by reversing the direction of every arrow to point upwards.
Replace the label 'a11' on the self-loop of node S1 with the value '0.2'.
Create a new connection between node a4 and node t using a curved line.
Change the solid line of the upward-pointing arrow labeled 'bded' to a dashed line style.
Delete the dashed line labeled with an underlined x at the bottom of the diagram.
Label the empty central circle with a summation sign (Σ).
Restructure the probability tree by vertically swapping the two main branches, placing the path starting with $\bar{A}$ at the top and the path starting with $A$ at the bottom.
Swap the vertical positions of the "Anger" node and the "Gratitude" node.
Replace the text label "Element's subscript order" at the top right with "Index Calculation Flow".
Reverse the direction of all connectors to depict the flow going from the bottom node up to the top node.
Introduce a feedback loop by adding a curved arrow that goes from the output of block D back to the input of block B.
Increase the line width of the edges forming the path from x1 to y1, y1 to x2, and x2 to y2.
Shift the red state labels ($s_1=00$ through $s_4=11$) on the left side horizontally to the right, placing them closer to the first column of nodes ($t_1$).
Shift the 'Variables selection' rectangle to the right to better balance the space between the layers.
Delete the node labeled '3,4' located at the bottom right of the graph.
Delete the innermost red oval labeled 'Körper' from the diagram.
Remove the 'output_tlast' signal from the interface.
Insert the label "R =" to the left of the resulting vector array on the right side.
Change the line style of the curved blue arrow connecting node 'b' to node 'j' to be dashed.
Change the line style of the arrows originating from the error terms ($e_1$ through $e_k$) to be dashed.
Update the value of the label on the vertical edge from '1/1' to '0/1'.
Shift the right-hand 'Name1' container in the bottom section further to the right to widen the gap between the two groups.
Remove the blue arrow that connects the 'Val1' boxes in the bottom 'Name1' section.
Reorganize the hierarchy by moving the two 'child' nodes to become dependents of the 'left' node instead of the 'right' node.
Create a new directed edge connecting node F3 to node B1 in the right-hand graph.
Delete the circle node labeled '2' situated on the right side of the tree.
Reposition the bottom horizontal line labeled B' upwards to decrease the gap between it and the twisted wires above.
Insert a title 'Tree Decomposition Levels' centered at the top of the image.
Change the geometry of all the state nodes in the diagram from circles to squares.
Invert the direction of all transition arrows in the diagram to represent the probabilities converging onto the central state t_i rather than emanating from it.
Create a new transition from state C to state E with the label '0'.
Swap the entire left branch (where x ≤ 5) with the right branch (where x ≥ 6) to mirror the decision tree's structure.
Expand the chart by adding a new rectangular node labeled 'G' positioned to the right of node 'F' to extend the alphabetical sequence.
Shift the position of the label $v_1$ to the right side of the vector's arrowhead.
Delete the yellow dashed grouping box labeled 'Theoretical analysis' on the left side of the chart.
Move the 'Test' and 'Train' labels, along with their horizontal dimension lines, from the top of the diagram to the bottom, positioning them below the 'Fold-k' label.
Find every instance of 'Convolutoin layer' in the yellow blocks and correct the spelling to 'Convolution layer'.
Simplify the forward path of the control system by merging the 'Planta de corriente' and 'Planta de Tensão' blocks into a single block labeled 'Unified Plant Model'.
Make the font bold for the last non-zero number in each row (1, 3, 6, and 10).
Delete the text label "Add two squares" situated below the middle bar with the red segments.
Increase the vertical gap between the third row and the fourth row of the matrix to visually separate the upper and lower sections.
Remove the pink cylindrical shape located at the bottom of the diagram.
Change the line style of the red dash-dotted arrows to solid.
Shift the central 'F' block and the components to its right further rightwards to increase the horizontal spacing after the first column of H gates.
Reposition the 'My Technique' node so that it sits to the right of 'Technique-2 [22-25]', aligning it horizontally with the other children of 'Type-3'.
Update the background color of the green region labeled 'T' to a light blue.
Shift the label "Content D" vertically upwards to create more space between the text and the top point of the triangle.
Systematically add diagonal edges to all the quadrilateral regions between the inner and outer rings to transform the graph topology into a triangulated structure.
Restructure the diagram to eliminate the shared dependency between the branches by splitting the 'T 2' node into two separate instances—one exclusively for 'char1' and another for 'char 2'.
Reverse the direction of all arrows in the graph to depict the backward flow of the system.
Change the line style of the two curved arrows connecting $S_2$ and $S_3$, labeled $d_1$ and $d_2$, to be dashed.
Add a text label reading 'Center Frequency: 915 MHz' near the red wireless signal lines between the Reader and Tag.
Reverse the direction of all the arrows in the diagram to simulate a backward flow of information.
Add a blue text label 'lo' above the upper curved arrow that points from node 2 to node bbb.
Delete the mathematical equations from inside all the blue hidden layer nodes.
Change the dashed circle surrounding the nodes to a solid line.
Insert a centered title at the top of the image that reads '5-Fold Cross-Validation Process'.
Delete the 'EMS' node located in the column on the left side.
Delete the label "c ≤ x" located next to the long vertical arrow on the left side of the diagram.
Delete the node labeled 'Data 2.4' located at the bottom left of the diagram.
Change the font color of all the green text labels in the diagram to black.
Invert the logical flow of the entire diagram by reversing the direction of every arrow.
Draw a new line connecting the blue node ν2 in the G^P group to the green node ν3 in the G^Q group on the left side of the diagram.
Insert a text label 'Root' with an arrow pointing to the top node ($x_1 < 0.75$) of the decision tree in subfigure (a).
Delete the direct arrow connecting Variable A to Variable B, which is labeled with the letter 'C'.
Structurally simplify the feedback loop by merging the 'Cost of the error' and 'Optimiser' blocks into a single block labeled 'Learning Update'.
Reposition the text label "Activation" so that it is placed directly below the square node containing "g", instead of above it.
Add the label '$\omega_{0}^{(2)}$' alongside the vertical arrow connecting the bias node to the summation unit.
Please move the label 'C' slightly upwards to create more space between the letter and the top vertex of the triangle.
Change the border style of the yellow 'Received by server' and 'Transmitted from server' containers from dashed to solid lines.
Move the black hexagon labeled 'Model' in the third section further to the right to create more separation from the central circle.
Restructure the list of directories by moving the 'tex' branch to the very top, so it appears as the first child of 'texmf'.
Add a new leaf node labeled 'D' connected to node 'B' in the middle tree.
Reverse the direction of every arrow in the diagram to fundamentally invert the process flow from decomposition to composition.
Delete the label '$O_2$' situated in the top region between the dashed lines.
Merge the three sequential nodes 'Stage 1', 'Stage 2', and 'Stage 3' on the left side into a single consolidated process block labeled 'Integrated SNHT Stages'.
Change the fill color of the three diamond-shaped decision nodes to a light orange.
Delete the red arrow connecting the title area to the box labeled X.
Draw a solid directional arrow from circle s4 to circle s3 and label it '1'.
Increase the vertical spacing between the nodes u1, u2, and u3 inside the right-hand dashed group to reduce crowding.
Draw a new edge connecting node A and node B in the graph labeled 'Clique 1'.
Relocate the label 'H' to the bottom right corner inside the bounding box.
Delete the text label 'Estimated parameter' and the associated theta symbol located beneath the blue hidden layer nodes.
Add a vertical arrow pointing downwards from the 'Inference Engine' block to the 'Knowledge Base' block.
Increase the horizontal spacing between the two central 'Now' nodes to create a larger gap between the left and right tree structures.
Insert a bold title 'Binary Classification Decision Tree' centered at the top of the diagram.
Insert a new green box labeled 'Final Output' to the right of the 'DMFT loop' block.
Relocate the plate label '10' from the bottom right to the top right corner within the rectangular boundary.
Transform the network topology into a star graph centered on X1 by removing all black edges connecting the other nodes.
Add a text label 'Bitstream' to the arrow connecting the 'Video encoder' block to the 'Network' block.
Place a new label '$v_k$' above the arrow connecting the 'Filter' and 'Angle' blocks.
Shift the Beta and Gamma boxes upwards so they align horizontally with the top of the Alpha box.
Add the label 'No Change' above the double arrow in the bottom sequence.
Increase the vertical spacing between the teal timeline axis and the upper row of red nodes x, y, and z.
Change the line style of the red boundary box on the right side to be solid instead of dashed.
Reposition the 'No' label to the center of the vertical segment of the return arrow on the left.
Delete the self-loop arrow attached to the top of node 5 in the left structure.
Flip the entire diagram horizontally.
Update the lines connecting the nodes to be dashed rather than solid.
Consolidate the 'Resources' and 'Sensors' nodes into a single unified block labeled 'Infrastructure' to abstract the specific details.
Change the border style of the grouping box labeled 'Desc' from dashed to solid.
Apply a light gray background fill to all the square data nodes containing numbers.
Change the color of the small subscript annotations (x0, x1) inside the first matrix from blue to red.
Shift the leftmost vertical stack containing 'c' and 'g' to the right, placing it closer to the central stack.
Draw a line connecting node a12 and node a13.
Delete the curved arrow pointing from I back to S in the bottom diagram.
Place a title label 'Digital Logic Unit' centered at the top of the diagram.
Change the shape of the node labeled '0' from a circle to a square.
Insert a thick, right-pointing arrow in the white space between panel (A) and panel (B) to indicate the direction of the process.
Move the numerical label '3' to the center of the empty circle node directly below it.
Delete the rightmost white node labeled 'Text' from the bottom row.
Add the text label 'Output' at the end of the arrow pointing right from the s0 block.
Change the fill color of the light green background region covering steps 1, 2, and 3 to light pink.
Insert a new blue square labeled 'F' in the right red column, positioning it directly below the 'D' block.
Change the background color of the two tall vertical rectangles from red to light gray.
Add a curved arrow pointing from B back to A to create a bidirectional flow.
Move the '$N_x$' label to the right side of the large recurring block container.
Restructure the diagram into two disjoint sets by separating circle A and circle B to eliminate the intersection.
Increase the horizontal spacing between the three nodes to distinctively separate the steps.
Increase the horizontal spacing between the Input, Hidden, and Output layers to make the diagram wider and the connections less crowded.
Change the shape of the nodes in the input layer from circles to squares.
Increase the vertical spacing between each level of the tree hierarchy to reduce the visual density.
Shift the position of the node labeled 'S' further to the right to increase the horizontal spacing between it and node 'L'.
Place a text label reading 'Group 1' centered above the left column of node pairs.
Remove the directed edge connecting node 'a' to node 'd'.
